title: True same-lap ranking expert with CatBoost query losses
summary: Current code only post-processes classifier scores into query-aware ranks; it does not train a model with a ranking objective.
rationale: Current code only post-processes classifier scores into query-aware ranks; it does not train a model with a ranking objective. CatBoost supports pairwise and groupwise ranking losses with `GroupId`, which is a more direct fit for choosing which drivers on a given race-lap are most likely to pit next.
implementation_hint: Create groups by `(Year,Race,LapNumber)`. Train a separate CatBoost ranker with `PairLogit`, `YetiRankPairwise`, or `QueryCrossEntropy`; label positives as drivers who pit next lap within the same group. Start with pair generation limited to positive-vs-negative pairs inside each group, then blend the ranker output with the existing classifier ensemble by regime or disagreement.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering within crowded race-laps, especially when absolute probabilities are noisy but intra-lap ordering is learnable.
risk: Very easy to overfit sparse groups or distort global calibration. Small groups and all-negative groups need careful handling, and ranking outputs should be blended as ranks or z-scores rather than treated as calibrated probabilities.
